reasoning: The value on Oct 24 closed at 5.06, which serves as the likely starting point for comparison. This is a local trough relative to recent weeks (averaging 5.15), setting a low bar for future yield increases. The FRED statistical model assigns a 70% chance of an increase by Nov 2, supported by seasonality and a slight upward medium-term trend despite recent short-term downward momentum. Adding confidence to a short-term yield increase or stabilization is the upcoming ECB meeting on Oct 30, where consensus holds that the ECB will pause rate cuts (maintaining the 2.00% benchmark), taking away an immediate catalyst for lower yields. In the medium-to-long term, LBO/M&A issuance is forecast to widen credit spreads, keeping the overall probability of being strictly greater than 5.06 higher than 50%. As the forecast horizon extends to January 2026, the variance outweighs our ability to project short-term policy and economic conditions, pulling the probabilities smoothly closer to 0.5.
